trust_assumption: Network and EDR defenses trust that traffic matching a configurable, attacker-defined profile (URIs, headers, sleep, jitter) is indistinguishable from a benign application, and that one signature describes one tool.
violation: Malleable command-and-control frameworks reshape every byte of the beacon — request shape, encoding, sleep cadence — so signatures and behavioral baselines built for one campaign never match the next, laundering implant traffic as ordinary application chatter.

techniques.name: Malleable beacon frameworks (Cobalt Strike / Sliver / Mythic / Havoc / Brute Ratel / Empire)

trust_assumption: The resolver and firewall trust recursive DNS as naming infrastructure that must always be allowed out, not as a bidirectional data channel.
violation: Commands and responses are tunneled inside DNS query/answer fields (TXT/NULL/CNAME labels), smuggling a full C2 link through a port nobody blocks because resolution conformance is mistaken for benignity.

techniques.name: DNS tunneling (iodine / dnscat2 / DNS over recursive resolvers)

trust_assumption: The firewall trusts ICMP echo as a diagnostic/keepalive protocol whose payload is meaningless padding, not a carrier for arbitrary data.
violation: C2 traffic is encoded into the data portion of ICMP echo request/reply packets, tunneling a control channel through ping because the inspector validates the type field, not the payload.

techniques.name: ICMP covert channel (icmptunnel / ptunnel / echo-payload C2)

trust_assumption: The TLS inspector trusts the visible SNI / outer Host as the true destination, assuming the front domain seen on the wire is where the connection actually terminates behind a shared CDN.
violation: The TLS SNI presents a reputable fronted domain while the inner HTTP Host routes to attacker infrastructure on the same CDN, so reputation and SNI-based allow-listing pass traffic to a destination the inspector never saw.

trust_assumption: Egress filtering trusts popular collaboration SaaS domains (Slack/Discord/Telegram/Teams) as universally benign and rarely inspected, since blocking them breaks legitimate business.
violation: Implants poll and post to Slack/Discord/Telegram bot APIs as their C2 transport, riding the platform's TLS and reputation so beacon traffic blends into sanctioned chat usage.

techniques.name: Web-service C2 via chat platforms (Slack / Discord / Telegram bot APIs)

trust_assumption: The defender trusts that traffic shape, TLS fingerprint (JA3/JA3S), and apparent source server are stable identifiers it can fingerprint and attribute as benign.
violation: Redirectors, malleable C2 profiles, and randomized/spoofed TLS fingerprints decouple the beacon's observable shape from its real origin, so JA3 baselining and infrastructure attribution chase a façade instead of the team server.

trust_assumption: DLP and egress controls trust uploads to sanctioned, reputable file-sharing SaaS (Dropbox / Google Drive / Mega) as authorized business activity not requiring content inspection.
violation: Stolen data is POSTed over HTTPS to a trusted cloud-storage provider, so the destination's reputation and the encrypted channel launder a bulk exfil as a routine sanctioned upload.

techniques.name: Exfiltration to trusted cloud storage (Dropbox / Drive / Mega via HTTPS POST)

trust_assumption: The resolver trusts outbound DNS queries as lookups whose subdomain labels carry only names to be resolved, never the payload being stolen.
violation: Data is encoded into query labels and leaked to an attacker-controlled authoritative server one chunk per lookup, exfiltrating through a protocol that is always permitted and rarely content-inspected.

techniques.name: DNS exfiltration (encoded subdomain labels to attacker NS)

trust_assumption: The inspector trusts a carrier file's declared type and visible meaning — an image, audio clip, or document is what it appears to be — and ignores its lossless bit-level capacity.
violation: Secret data is embedded in the redundant bits of an innocuous carrier (LSB images, audio, document metadata) so a benign-looking file smuggles the payload past DLP that only reads the surface content.

techniques.name: Steganographic exfiltration (LSB image/audio embedding, doc metadata)

trust_assumption: DLP trusts that small, encrypted, rate-limited transfers spread over time are unrelated events, not slices of one dataset, because it inspects each flow in isolation without temporal correlation.
violation: A large dataset is encrypted, split into sub-threshold chunks, and trickled out under size/rate limits, defeating volume-based and content-based detection that never reassembles the pieces across time.

trust_assumption: The endpoint trusts a mounted USB / removable volume as authorized local storage whose write operations are ordinary file activity inside the security boundary.
violation: Data is copied to removable media and physically carried out, exfiltrating across an air gap or around network DLP entirely because the device inherits the trust of a logged-on user at a sanctioned workstation.

trust_assumption: Every domain-joined resource trusts a Domain Admin / Tier-0 principal absolutely — its tickets and tokens are honored everywhere without question as the root of directory authority.
violation: Once a Tier-0 credential or Kerberos golden ticket is held, the attacker exercises unconditional control over all domain resources, the forged or stolen identity granting impact across the entire estate.

techniques.name: Domain Admin / Tier-0 takeover (DCSync, golden ticket, full estate control)

trust_assumption: Crown-jewel data stores trust that any principal holding a valid authorization grant has a legitimate business need for the PII, IP, or financial records it can read.
violation: An over-broad or compromised grant is exercised to reach and stage the organization's most sensitive datasets, the bare presence of authorization mistaken for entitlement to the crown jewels.

techniques.name: Crown-jewel data access (PII / IP / financial record staging)

trust_assumption: A business workflow trusts that an authenticated operator acting at the right step and time intends the operation, with no out-of-band check that the state transition is legitimate.
violation: An attacker abuses authorized access to halt, corrupt, or reorder a critical business process — stopping pipelines, altering state, denying availability — because the workflow conflates authentication with legitimate intent at that moment.

trust_assumption: The financial workflow trusts that a request approved by an authenticated operator with the right role is a legitimate, intended transaction requiring no independent confirmation of the counterparty.
violation: An attacker with operator access or session control initiates and self-approves a fraudulent wire / payment, the authenticated approval laundering a theft into a sanctioned transaction the controls were built to honor.

techniques.name: Fraudulent wire transfer / payment fraud path
